title: System Component Inventory vs Observed Network Assets

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 CM-8 ‚Äî Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 CM-8 is enforced ‚Äî Splunk UC-22.14.58: System Component Inventory vs Observed Network Assets.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.14.58' running on infoblox:dhcp or msdns:debug, cmdb_hosts.csv, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted ‚Äî SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Infoblox (TA) or DHCP logs, CMDB lookup
spl:
index=net sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp earliest=-24h
| stats latest(mac) as mac, latest(fingerprint) as fp by hostname, ip
| lookup cmdb_hosts.csv hostname OUTPUT owner, classification
| where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")
description: Reconciles CMDB-managed systems with active DHCP/DNS or scan observations for CM-8 system component inventory.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes net and sourcetypes infoblox:dhcp. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Normalize hostname case; (2) dedupe NAT; (3) feed discovery to CMDB; (4) monthly rogue asset report. Indexes required: `index=net`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp`; Lookups: `cmdb_hosts`, `cmdb_hosts.csv`.
visualization: Table (unknown assets), Map (subnet), Single value (count). Key fields: `fp`, `hostname`, `ip`, `mac`.
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern ‚Äî correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Infoblox (TA) or DHCP logs, CMDB lookup.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `infoblox:dhcp` or `msdns:debug`, `cmdb_hosts.csv`.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
(1) Normalize hostname case; (2) dedupe NAT; (3) feed discovery to CMDB; (4) monthly rogue asset report.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=net sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp earliest=-24h
| stats latest(mac) as mac, latest(fingerprint) as fp by hostname, ip
| lookup cmdb_hosts.csv hostname OUTPUT owner, classification
| where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")
```

Understanding this SPL

**System Component Inventory vs Observed Network Assets (CM-8)** ‚Äî Reconciles CMDB-managed systems with active DHCP/DNS or scan observations for CM-8 system component inventory.

Documented **Data sources**: `infoblox:dhcp` or `msdns:debug`, `cmdb_hosts.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Infoblox (TA) or DHCP logs, CMDB lookup. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: net; **sourcetype**: infoblox:dhcp. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=net, sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by hostname, ip** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unknown assets), Map (subnet), Single value (count)
grandmaExplanation: We watch for reconciles cmdb-managed systems with active dhcp/dns or scan observations for system component inventory. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
